original_hypothesis.title: ERC20Airdrop2 claim lacks per-user cap; repeated valid leaves across multiple roots could accumulate claimedAmount (if owner reuses eligibility)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ERC20Airdrop2.claim() stores claimedAmount[user] += amount, while replay protection is keyed by hash = keccak256(abi.encode("CLAIM_TAIKO_AIRDROP", abi.encode(user, amount))). If the owner updates merkleRoot to include multiple distinct leaves for the same user with different amounts across successive roots (and different hashes), the same user could claim multiple times, accumulating claimedAmount beyond intended allocation. This is not possible without owner-controlled merkleRoot changes, but is a realistic operational failure mode when running multiple airdrop rounds on the same contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Teams often reuse deployments and rotate merkle roots for subsequent rounds. Without an explicit per-user max entitlement, operational mistakes in root generation or root rotation policy can lead to over-allocation and vault drain.
deployment_assumptions: Same ERC20Airdrop2 instance is reused across multiple airdrop rounds by changing merkleRoot
Merkle trees for later rounds include the same user again with new (user,amount) pairs
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: MerkleClaimable.setConfig exists and is unconstrained; ERC20Airdrop2.claim increments claimedAmount[user] and claim uniqueness is per leaf hash, not per user.
preconditions: Owner updates merkleRoot between rounds on the same contract instance
User appears in multiple merkle roots with different (user, amount) pairs
Vault has sufficient allowance/balance to honor withdrawals

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Per-leaf replay protection combined with cumulative claimedAmount can permit multi-round accumulation if contract is reused.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Depends on owner-controlled setConfig/root rotation and tree construction discipline.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Across multiple setConfig updates (multi-round airdrop operations)

operational_questions: Will ERC20Airdrop2 be reused for multiple rounds or is each round a new deployment?
Does the merkle generation pipeline enforce per-user caps across rounds?
Is there an on-chain max claim per address tracked elsewhere?
validation_requirements: Review airdrop operational plan and historical/test scripts for root rotation
Attempt a multi-root scenario in tests to confirm cumulative behavior
Check vault allowance and accounting expectations

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait for root update (new round) that includes attacker address again with a new amount
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Submit claim for each eligible (user,amount) leaf across rounds
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Withdraw during withdrawal window to extract total accumulated claimedAmount

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: None required beyond root rotation behavior; but root creation is an admin process
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Over-distribution of airdrop funds; potential vault insolvency
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Airdrop distribution accounting and vault funding

execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: Low for claimant once misconfiguration occurs
execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Must claim within each configured claim window

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Repeated setConfig calls and merkleRoot changes; same addresses appearing in new datasets
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Addresses claiming in multiple rounds on same contract instance

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
